title: Battery widget multiple columns

posts.author: nickomexom (Nick)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi All,

I have quite a large board with hundreds of pulses and 4 main status columns and 4 sub item status columns.

I’m able to create dashboard views with battery widgets but each battery widget can show only a max of one main status column and one subitem column.

Surely there is a way to track via the battery widget the overall status of more than one column? What am I missing here???

I have progress columns for each pulse which does the job for each pulse, but I can’t seem to link the battery widget to the progress column which I believe would do the trick.

How on earth do you track the status of all tasks on the entire board? Can this not be done?

posts.author: malia.monday (Malia)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Nick! The Battery Widget currently only supports a single Status Column per board due to its functionality of vertically calculating the progress of a board.

As a workaround, the Overview Widget can be used instead for pulling in data from a Progress Column.
